title: Review and setlist: The Strokes’ appeal isn’t ‘hard to explain’ even 20 years later
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The Strokes performed at TD Garden in Boston, their first appearance in the city in nearly 20 years.
The concert is part of their 'Reality Awaits' tour, promoting their upcoming seventh album, set for July release.
The setlist included tracks from various albums, with new singles like 'Going Shopping' and 'Falling Out of Love.'

title: Davio’s Northern Italian Steakhouse to close Chestnut Hill restaurant
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Davio's Northern Italian Steakhouse in Chestnut Hill will close on June 28 after 13 years in operation.
The closure is due to a change in ownership at the adjoining Showcase SuperLux, resulting in no lease agreement.
Owner Steve DiFillippo is exploring new locations on the South Shore; Davio's flagship remains in Back Bay.

title: National group alleges ‘anti-Muslim hate campaign’ in Newton
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: CAIR-MA claims an anti-Muslim campaign arose after the Eid al-Adha event at Newton City Hall.
A letter from 17 people, including 10 from Newton, questioned event participants' links to terrorism.
Newton paused rentals of public spaces while reevaluating policies after the controversy.

title: Boston Dynamics seeks to double local headcount with Greater Boston expansion
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Boston Dynamics plans a $100 million expansion in Waltham, doubling its local workforce and creating 1,250 jobs by 2033.
The new 300,000-square-foot facility will consolidate operations and enhance robotics and AI capabilities near their current headquarters.
The Healey-Driscoll Administration granted Boston Dynamics $25 million to support the project, which begins phased relocation in mid-2027.

title: 9-year-old seriously injured after falling from window in Hanson
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A 9-year-old boy fell from a third-story window in Hanson, sustaining serious injuries.
Emergency responders arrived on Phillips Street at 12:34 p.m., providing immediate medical attention.
The boy was airlifted to a Boston trauma center; Hanson police are investigating the fall.

title: Teso Life is opening a 20,000-square-foot Downtown Crossing store Friday
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Teso Life is opening a 20,000-square-foot store at 459 Washington St. in Boston's Downtown Crossing on June 26.
The Asian lifestyle chain will sell a variety of imported snacks, beauty products, toys, and home goods.
Teso Life chose the location due to below-market rent, and the store opens daily from 11 a.m. to 9 p.m.

title: Your next Uber or Lyft ride in Boston could be a taxi
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Boston launches a one-year pilot allowing taxis to accept Uber and Lyft ride requests.
The program aims to increase transportation options and boost taxi driver earnings.
Riders will pay Uber and Lyft prices; airport and special event rides excluded from the pilot.

title: Photos: Here are the best scenes from the England-Ghana World Cup matchup at ‘Boston Stadium’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: England and Ghana played to a 0-0 draw at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough in front of over 64,000 fans.
Ghanaian fans were highly vocal, outmatching the enthusiastic support from England during the match at Boston Stadium.
Jerome Opoku and Declan Rice were standout players, while Harry Kane missed his only chance on goal for England.

title: City councilor proposes making 3 a.m. last call permanent in Boston
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Councilor Brian Worrell proposes permanently extending last call in Boston to 3 a.m. for bars and restaurants.
A recently signed bill allows alcohol sales until 3 a.m., with Boston already participating in the pilot program.
Opposition arises, with Boston Police citing increased crime risks from extended drinking hours.

title: Motorcyclist falls 65 feet to riverbank after crashing into guardrail in N.H.
author: John Waller
bullets: A motorcyclist was severely injured after crashing into a guardrail on Interstate 293 in Manchester, N.H.
The rider was ejected over the guardrail, falling approximately 65 feet to the Merrimack River bank.
First responders accessed the victim using boats; he suffered multi-system trauma and was taken for further care.
